text.cms:hero.introduction::text-0: A premier commercial law firm offering strategic legal advisory and dispute resolution to growth-oriented enterprises, multinational corporations, and industry leaders.
text.cms:firm.paragraphOne::text-0: KDH Advocates LLP, formerly Ken, Daniel & Henry Advocates, combines legal excellence with commercial insight to deliver tailored solutions aligned with each client's strategic objectives.
text.cms:firm.paragraphTwo::text-0: Every matter is treated with the utmost diligence and intellectual rigour. We see legal challenges not merely as problems to be solved, but as opportunities to advance and safeguard our clients' business interests.
text.#main>section:nth-of-type(2)>div:nth-of-: To deliver with integrity and excellence, empowering businesses to thrive while setting new standards in the legal profession through innovation and unwavering dedication.
To be a globally recognised law firm, setting the standard for excellence, integrity, and client success.
text.#main>section:nth-of-type(3)>div:nth-of-: The confidence our clients place in us as stewards of their most critical matters.
The uncompromising standard of honesty, fairness, and ethics that governs every engagement.
Outcomes that protect, enhance, and advance our clients' business and legal interests.
The highest levels of competence, diligence, and respect in all that we do.
Timely, transparent, and practical advice that clients can rely on.
Value-driven legal services that balance quality with efficiency.
text.#main>section:nth-of-type(4)>div:nth-of-: We serve as long-term partners, offering client-centred counsel without compromising quality. Our advice is strategic, practical, and built to endure.
Local and international experience across corporate, tax, real estate, trade, and dispute resolution.
Proven capability in cross-border transactions, investments, and regulatory compliance.
Bespoke strategies designed around each client's unique needs and goals.
Technology and modern legal tools that enable efficient, forward-looking service.
A client-first approach built on trust, collaboration, and long-term relationships.
Deep knowledge across finance, energy, healthcare, technology, and other priority sectors.
text.#main>section:nth-of-type(5)>div:nth-of-: Through strong working relationships with reputable partner law firms, we provide coordinated, on-the-ground legal support with consistent service, efficiency, and deep local insight.
text.#main>section:nth-of-type(6)>div:nth-of-: Full-service commercial practice
Our practice spans the transactions, regulation, disputes, people, property, and cross-border opportunities that shape modern enterprise.
Corporate & Commercial
Corporate and commercial services designed to support businesses at every stage of growth.
Business formation, company registration, governance, and corporate structuring
Mergers, acquisitions, restructuring, and corporate reorganisations
Shareholder agreements, joint ventures, and strategic partnerships
Commercial contracts and transactions across sectors
Corporate governance and sector-specific regulatory compliance
Insolvency, liquidation, restructuring, and business rescue
Dispute Resolution
Strategic representation in domestic and international courts and tribunals, with a thorough assessment of legal and reputational risk.
Commercial litigation and arbitration
Employment and labour relations disputes
Corporate and shareholder disputes
Constitutional and administrative law litigation
Land, environmental justice, debt recovery, and insolvency litigation
Insurance claims, defence, recovery, and related claims
Employment & Labour Relations
Advice for employers and employees across the full spectrum of workplace matters.
Employment contracts, policies, benefits, and compensation structures
Workplace disputes, litigation, health, and safety compliance
Redundancies, terminations, and restructuring
Collective bargaining and industrial relations
Kenyan employment law compliance
Real Estate & Construction
Transactional, development, investment, finance, and dispute-resolution counsel for commercial and residential property.
Acquisitions, sales, conveyancing, development, and joint ventures
Commercial and residential leases
Land use planning, zoning, and environmental compliance
Real estate finance and securitisation
Property disputes and construction contract management
Banking, Finance & Capital Markets
Finance and capital-markets advisory for institutions, lenders, borrowers, investors, issuers, underwriters, and market participants.
Corporate and structured finance transactions
Syndicated lending, project finance, and asset financing
Securitisation and enforcement of securities
Banking regulatory compliance and risk management
Equity and debt offerings, private equity, venture capital, and private placements
Legal due diligence, restructuring, and corporate governance
International Trade & Investment
Strategic counsel for businesses engaged in cross-border trade and investment.
International trade agreements and compliance
Foreign direct investment and multinational investment structuring
Import-export regulation, customs compliance, and trade finance
Risk mitigation and security documentation
Community benefit-sharing agreements
External market access training and investor aftercare
Climate Change & Sustainability
Legal support across environmental responsibility, sustainable investment, climate finance, and ESG.
Environmental impact assessment and compliance
Corporate sustainability strategies and sustainable investment regulation
Social-impact projects and legal frameworks
Climate policy and carbon-trading regulation
Climate finance, project development, policy advocacy, and ESG reporting
Technology, Media, Telecoms & IP
Future-ready legal solutions at the intersection of technology, media, telecommunications, and intellectual property.
Data protection, cybersecurity, and breach response
Patents, trademarks, copyrights, licensing, and enforcement
Technology, software, telecom, content, and media contracts
Telecom licensing, infrastructure, and regulatory compliance
Startup funding, scaling, regulation, and IP litigation
Tax
Practical advisory, compliance, structuring, and dispute-resolution solutions for a changing tax landscape.
Corporate tax compliance, planning, and structuring
VAT, income tax, customs duty, and transfer pricing
International tax compliance
Tax disputes, litigation, risk assessment, and mitigation
Tax policy advocacy
Immigration
Tailored immigration advice for individuals, businesses, and multinational organisations establishing a presence in Kenya.
Work permits and residency applications
Business and investor visa processing
Citizenship and naturalisation advice
Corporate immigration compliance
Deportation appeals and legal representation
Energy & Infrastructure
Trusted counsel for structuring and executing complex projects across Kenya's growing energy and infrastructure sectors.
Energy law and regulatory compliance
Power purchase agreements and project finance
Oil, gas, and renewable energy advice
Public-private partnerships and government projects
Infrastructure contracts, concession agreements, and social-impact compliance
text.#main>section:nth-of-type(7)>div:nth-of-: Deep sector expertise allows our team to understand the commercial context behind every legal question.
Regulation, transactions, capital, and risk management.
Project development, licensing, compliance, and contracts.
Acquisition, development, leasing, and project delivery.
Cybersecurity, licensing, IP, and digital regulation.
Public projects, financing, and concession agreements.
Approvals, compliance, clinical trials, and transactions.
Trade, supply chains, contracting, and product liability.
Land rights, finance, and cross-border trade.
Environmental compliance, ESG, and responsible growth.
text.#team-title::text-0: People who pair legal depth with
text.#main>section:nth-of-type(8)>div:nth-of-: Our lawyers combine technical legal excellence with practical commercial insight to deliver advice that is strategic, innovative, and results-driven.
International law, trade, investment & climate finance
text.cms:contact.introduction::text-0: We prioritise a face-to-face first consultation so that we can understand your concerns, objectives, and instructions in full.
text.cms:contact.office::text-0: IPS Building, 1st Floor, Kimathi Street, Nairobi
text.#contact>form:nth-of-type(1)>div:nth-of-: Complete the details below and we will prepare an email to the KDH team.
text.#contact>form:nth-of-type(1)>label:nth-o: I consent to KDH Advocates using my information to respond to this enquiry.
text.footer:nth-of-type(1)>div:nth-of-type(1): Strategic legal advisory and dispute resolution for business across Africa.

text.#profile-yvonne>div:nth-of-type(1)>div:n: Yvonne is the Managing Partner at KDH Advocates and Head of the firm's Commercial and Real Estate Practice. She has extensive experience advising local and international clients on complex real estate, construction, and finance matters. Her work includes structuring development projects, arranging real estate finance, implementing securitisation frameworks, and guiding high-net-worth individuals through estate planning strategies.

Her experience at Ecobank gave her a strong foundation in securitisation and banking regulatory compliance. She works closely with financial institutions and SACCOs on compliant frameworks for securitisation, recovery, and governance, while also advising on structured finance, syndicated lending, project finance, asset financing, enforcement of securities, and capital raising.

Her real estate practice spans acquisitions, sales and conveyancing, property development and joint ventures, commercial and residential leases, land use planning, zoning, environmental compliance, construction contract management, and property disputes.

Yvonne combines transactional, advisory, and dispute-resolution expertise to serve property developers, financial institutions, and investment companies as a trusted adviser in Kenya's real estate and construction landscape.
text.#profile-brance>div:nth-of-type(1)>div:n: Brance is a Founding Partner at KDH Advocates and a registered Patent Agent. He leads the firm's Dispute Resolution Department, representing national and international clients in complex, high-stakes commercial, construction, insolvency, and intellectual property matters.

He has acted in substantial arbitrations concerning electrical equipment and advertising boards, a major Nairobi construction project, and the development of a bespoke management system. He has also defended a microfinance institution against a multimillion-shilling employment claim.

His practice extends to intellectual property and real estate. Brance has advised in a significant hospitality trademark dispute, defended homeowners against fraudulent land claims, and guided clients through high-value property acquisitions in Nairobi.

He represents the Law Society of Kenya, Nairobi Branch, on the Supreme Court Bar-Bench Committee, Tax Liaison Committee, and Commercial Court Bar-Bench Committee. He also serves on the boards of Shield Forensics Kenya and International Christian Youthworks (ICY), Africa.
text.#profile-imanuel>div:nth-of-type(1)>div:: Imanuel leads the International Law and Trade Practice at KDH Advocates. He advises on cross-border transactions, international trade, and investment agreements, drawing on experience with EU export controls and sanctions in Brussels and trade-policy work supporting Kenya's Ministry of Industry, Trade, and Investments.

His international-organisation experience includes the Green Climate Fund, where he worked on grant, loan, and equity investment agreements for climate projects, and legal work with the UN's International Fund for Agricultural Development and World Food Programme in Rome.

Imanuel has advised across employment, mining, investment establishment, banking acquisitions, and supply-chain finance. His work includes due diligence on tax, intellectual property, shareholding, and regulatory compliance, as well as drafting and negotiating key transaction documents.

He serves as adjunct faculty at Kenyatta University School of Law, coordinates the TradeLab, and contributes to scholarship on the African Continental Free Trade Area and the East African Community Rule of Law Index. He is also Chairperson of the Kenyatta University School of Law Alumni Association.
text.#profile-lynne>div:nth-of-type(1)>div:nt: Lynne is an Associate in the Dispute Resolution Department. She works closely with the partners in complex litigation and arbitration before domestic and international courts and tribunals.
Her matters include commercial litigation and arbitration, corporate and shareholder disputes, employment and labour relations, constitutional and administrative law, land, and insolvency.
She brings a keen eye for detail and a strategic approach, combining efficient litigation with alternative mechanisms such as arbitration and mediation to secure the best outcomes for clients.
text.#profile-dorcas>div:nth-of-type(1)>div:n: Dorcas is an Associate in the Real Estate and Construction Practice. She supports property developers, financial institutions, and local and international investors through complex transactions and developments.

Her work spans acquisitions, sales and conveyancing, property development and joint ventures, commercial and residential leasing, land use planning, environmental compliance, real estate finance, securitisation, and construction contract management.
Dorcas approaches every matter with precision and a client-focused perspective, structuring transactions to minimise risk while maximising value.
text.#profile-scort>div:nth-of-type(1)>div:nt: Advocate of the High Court of Kenya
Scort advises employers and employees on employment contracts, workplace policies, compensation structures, and the full range of workplace issues.

His practice includes dispute resolution and litigation, health and safety compliance, redundancies, terminations, restructuring, collective bargaining, industrial relations, and Kenyan employment-law compliance.
He focuses on practical, balanced solutions to workplace challenges.
